updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (keywords only). Do not output explanations, bullets, or multiple queries—only the query string.

Reversibility constraints (strict):
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers present in the inputs. Do not introduce new facts or confirmed answers not in the inputs.
- If a candidate is uncertain or multiple candidates exist, keep that uncertainty or candidate set rather than collapsing to one name.

Behavioral repair guidance (small, conservative edits to recover missing support):
- Prefer answer-bearing pages by restoring missing relation tokens and precise type words. Combine the core anchor(s) from the inputs with a short relation token and a support-type token. Examples of patterns to prefer: university name + editor-in-chief + publication-type (journal / magazine / review); grantor canonical name + title name + relation token (bestow / decree / title) plus date if present; station call sign/frequency + location + PEP/Primary Entry Point/Emergency Alert System; source work title + primary artist + record label/released/label.
- Allow introducing candidate person names only when those names already appear in summary_1 or are explicitly suggested by first-hop support titles or roster/alumni pages referenced in summary_1; treat them as candidate anchors, not confirmed answers. Do not invent unrelated person names.
- Keep queries short and focused: core anchor(s) + 1–3 relation/type qualifiers that steer retrieval to answer-bearing pages (publication, credits, roster, label, station page, decree, credits, assistant director, etc.).

Preservation constraints (safety):
- Preserve concise anchor + relation behavior demonstrated by successful examples: use the subject or canonical name plus a clear relation verb/noun to locate the support page (for example: name + defeated + opponent title; name + Deputy President under + leader; film title + assistant director credits).
- Avoid web-search operators, complex Boolean syntax, and quoted search-programming; write plain keywords.

Output rules (repeat):
- Output exactly one compact BM25 keyword query string. Use concrete names, titles, aliases, and relation/type tokens present in the inputs. Do not invent facts.
rationale: Integrated several small, generalizable fixes observed in failed hops: biasing for publication/title pages for editor queries, pivoting to grantor canonical names for honor/title provenance, forcing station queries to use call sign/frequency+PEP terms, anchoring on original/source works for cover/parody label lookups, and allowing candidate names only when suggested by summary_1 or hop-1 support. Preserves successful concise anchor+relation pattern as a safety boundary and maintains strict no-new-facts reversibility.
absorbed_patterns: Bias query toward publication/journal/magazine tokens + university name + editor-in-chief for editorial staff retrieval
Pivot to grantor canonical name + title name + relation/date to find grantor-issued honors
Use station call sign/frequency + location + PEP/EAS tokens to target station entity pages
Anchor on original/source work title + primary artist + record label/released to find label info
Permit adding candidate person names only if present in summary_1 or explicit first-hop support
preserved_patterns: Concise anchor + relation tokens (subject + verb/noun) to retrieve support pages
Keep uncertainty and candidate sets instead of confirming answers
Preserve concrete names/titles/aliases from inputs and avoid inventing facts
Output only one compact keyword BM25 query
avoided_patterns: Introducing unrelated person names or noisy side entities
Retrieving generic campus/event/host pages instead of publication/credit/label pages
Restating the full question or producing overly generic role-only queries
Using web-search operators, quotes, OR-heavy Boolean syntax

Combine the core anchor(s) from the inputs with a short relation token and a support-type token. Examples of patterns to prefer: university name + editor-in-chief + publication-type (journal / magazine / review); grantor canonical name + title name + relation token (bestow / decree / title) plus date if present; station call sign/frequency + location + PEP/Primary Entry Point/Emergency Alert System; source work title + primary artist + record label/released/label.\n- Allow introducing candidate person names only when those names already appear in summary_1 or are explicitly suggested by first-hop support titles or roster/alumni pages referenced in summary_1; treat them as candidate anchors, not confirmed answers. Do not invent unrelated person names.\n- Keep queries short and focused: core anchor(s) + 1–3 relation/type qualifiers that steer retrieval to answer-bearing pages (publication, credits, roster, label, station page, decree, credits, assistant director, etc.).\n\nPreservation constraints (safety):\n- Preserve concise anchor + relation behavior demonstrated by successful examples: use the subject or canonical name plus a clear relation verb/noun to locate the support page (for example: name + defeated + opponent title; name + Deputy President under + leader; film title + assistant director credits).\n- Avoid web-search operators, complex Boolean syntax, and quoted search-programming; write plain keywords.\n\nOutput rules (repeat):\n- Output exactly one compact BM25 keyword query string. Use concrete names, titles, aliases, and relation/type tokens present in the inputs. Do not invent facts.",
  "rationale": "Integrated several small, generalizable fixes observed in failed hops: biasing for publication/title pages for editor queries, pivoting to grantor canonical names for honor/title provenance, forcing station queries to use call sign/frequency+PEP terms, anchoring on original/source works for cover/parody label lookups, and allowing candidate names only when suggested by summary_1 or hop-1 support. Preserves successful concise anchor+relation pattern as a safety boundary and maintains strict no-new-facts reversibility.",
  "absorbed_patterns": [
    "Bias query toward publication/journal/magazine tokens + university name + editor-in-chief for editorial staff retrieval",
    "Pivot to grantor canonical name + title name + relation/date to find grantor-issued honors",
    "Use station call sign/frequency + location + PEP/EAS tokens to target station entity pages",
    "Anchor on original/source work title + primary artist + record label/released to find label info",
    "Permit adding candidate person names only if present in summary_1 or explicit first-hop support"
  ],
  "preserved_patterns": [

    "Keep uncertainty and candidate sets instead of confirming answers",
    "Preserve concrete names/titles/aliases from inputs and avoid inventing facts",
    "Output only one compact keyword BM25 query"
  ],
  "avoided_patterns": [
    "Introducing unrelated person names or noisy side entities",
    "Retrieving generic campus/event/host pages instead of publication/credit/label pages",
    "Restating the full question or producing overly generic role-only queries",
    "Using web-search operators, quotes, OR-heavy Boolean syntax"
  ]
}

updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce exactly one compact second-hop BM25 search query string (keywords only). Do not output explanations, multiple queries, or JSON.

Mandatory reversibility constraints (preserve from input):
- Preserve task‑relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers present in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not invent new entities, aliases, facts, candidate answers, or support titles that are not present in the inputs, except as explicitly allowed below.
- If a candidate is uncertain or multiple candidates are present, keep that uncertainty / candidate set — do not collapse to a single confirmed answer.

Allowed, conservative relaxations (small, generalizable repairs):
- You may add specific person names or small candidate anchors only if they are explicitly recommended or implied by `summary_1` or by hop‑1 support titles (e.g., roster, alumni list, crew/credits page). Treat such added names as candidate anchors, not confirmed answers.

Behavioral steering rules (prefer concise relation/type tokens to reach answer‑bearing pages):
- Editor‑in‑chief (university‑affiliated): include the university name/alias from `summary_1`, the relation phrase editor‑in‑chief (or editor/editorial board), and a short publication token (journal, magazine, review, Annual Review) to bias toward title/publication pages.
- Grant/title provenance: when the missing support is a grantor, pivot to the grantor's canonical name (from the inputs) plus the title name and concise relation/date tokens (e.g., royal decree, granted, 30 December 1991) to find the grantor/title page.
- Radio station / PEP: favor station identifiers (call sign and/or frequency), location (state/city), and relation tokens (Primary Entry Point, PEP, Emergency Alert System, EAS).
- Parody/cover → source work: if the question links a parody/cover to an original work, make the original/source title (and primary artist) the primary anchor and add relation tokens like record label, label, released, released by.

Preservation/safety constraints (do not break nearby successful behaviors):
- Continue to prefer concise title/author + relation tokens when those recovered successful answers (examples: book title + author + defeated; film title + year + assistant director credits; person + Deputy President under + leader).
- Avoid web‑search operators or Boolean programs (no site:, OR lists, or quoted operator logic).

Output rule: output only one compact keyword BM25 query string that follows the above constraints and steering rules.
rationale: Incorporates minimal, generalizable repairs observed in failed hops: steer queries toward answer‑bearing pages by restoring missing relation words and short type tokens (publication, label, PEP, royal decree), allow adding candidate names only when summary/hop‑1 explicitly suggests them, and preserve reversible anchors and uncertainty. At the same time preserve successful C‑side behaviors (use of concise title/author + relation tokens, distribution tokens, film/year/credits phrasing) as safety boundaries.
absorbed_patterns: bias toward publication/title pages using a publication-type token + editor-in-chief
pivot to grantor canonical name + title + relation/date tokens for grant/title verification
prefer station call sign + frequency + location + PEP/EAS relation tokens
anchor second hop on original/source work + artist + 'record label'/'released' tokens
allow adding candidate person names only if explicitly suggested by summary_1 or hop-1 support titles
preserved_patterns: preserve named entities, titles, relations, dates and uncertainty markers from inputs
use concise title/author + relation tokens (e.g., defeated + author/title) as effective anchors
use distribution/region tokens for flora/fauna range questions
use film title + year + 'assistant director credits' phrasing to find crew pages
do not introduce unsupported facts; keep candidate sets when uncertain
avoided_patterns: introducing unrelated person names (e.g., noisy hosts) not supported by inputs
retrieving campus/student pages or facility/event pages instead of publication/title pages
collapsing multiple candidates into one confirmed answer
restating the full question or producing long explanatory queries
using web-search operators or Boolean/quoted operator syntax
raw:
{
  "updated_prompt": "Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce exactly one compact second-hop BM25 search query string (keywords only). Do not output explanations, multiple queries, or JSON.\n\nMandatory reversibility constraints (preserve from input):\n- Preserve task‑relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers present in `question` or `summary_1`.\n- Do not invent new entities, aliases, facts, candidate answers, or support titles that are not present in the inputs, except as explicitly allowed below.\n- If a candidate is uncertain or multiple candidates are present, keep that uncertainty / candidate set — do not collapse to a single confirmed answer.\n\nAllowed, conservative relaxations (small, generalizable repairs):\n- You may add specific person names or small candidate anchors only if they are explicitly recommended or implied by `summary_1` or by hop‑1 support titles (e.g., roster, alumni list, crew/credits page). Treat such added names as candidate anchors, not confirmed answers.\n\nBehavioral steering rules (prefer concise relation/type tokens to reach answer‑bearing pages):\n- Editor‑in‑chief (university‑affiliated): include the university name/alias from `summary_1`, the relation phrase editor‑in‑chief (or editor/editorial board), and a short publication token (journal, magazine, review, Annual Review) to bias toward title/publication pages.\n- Grant/title provenance: when the missing support is a grantor, pivot to the grantor's canonical name (from the inputs) plus the title name and concise relation/date tokens (e.g., royal decree, granted, 30 December 1991) to find the grantor/title page.\n- Radio station / PEP: favor station identifiers (call sign and/or frequency), location (state/city), and relation tokens (Primary Entry Point, PEP, Emergency Alert System, EAS).\n- Parody/cover → source work: if the question links a parody/cover to an original work, make the original/source title (and primary artist) the primary anchor and add relation tokens like record label, label, released, released by.\n\nPreservation/safety constraints (do not break nearby successful behaviors):\n- Continue to prefer concise title/author + relation tokens when those recovered successful answers (examples: book title + author + defeated; film title + year + assistant director credits; person + Deputy President under + leader).\n- Avoid web‑search operators or Boolean programs (no site:, OR lists, or quoted operator logic).\n\nOutput rule: output only one compact keyword BM25 query string that follows the above constraints and steering rules.",
  "rationale": "Incorporates minimal, generalizable repairs observed in failed hops: steer queries toward answer‑bearing pages by restoring missing relation words and short type tokens (publication, label, PEP, royal decree), allow adding candidate names only when summary/hop‑1 explicitly suggests them, and preserve reversible anchors and uncertainty. At the same time preserve successful C‑side behaviors (use of concise title/author + relation tokens, distribution tokens, film/year/credits phrasing) as safety boundaries.",
  "absorbed_patterns": [
    "bias toward publication/title pages using a publication-type token + editor-in-chief",
    "pivot to grantor canonical name + title + relation/date tokens for grant/title verification",
    "prefer station call sign + frequency + location + PEP/EAS relation tokens",
    "anchor second hop on original/source work + artist + 'record label'/'released' tokens",
    "allow adding candidate person names only if explicitly suggested by summary_1 or hop-1 support titles"
  ],
  "preserved_patterns": [
    "preserve named entities, titles, relations, dates and uncertainty markers from inputs",
    "use concise title/author + relation tokens (e.g., defeated + author/title) as effective anchors",
    "use distribution/region tokens for flora/fauna range questions",
    "use film title + year + 'assistant director credits' phrasing to find crew pages",
    "do not introduce unsupported facts; keep candidate sets when uncertain"
  ],
  "avoided_patterns": [
    "introducing unrelated person names (e.g., noisy hosts) not supported by inputs",
    "retrieving campus/student pages or facility/event pages instead of publication/title pages",
    "collapsing multiple candidates into one confirmed answer",
    "restating the full question or producing long explanatory queries",
    "using web-search operators or Boolean/quoted operator syntax"
  ]
}
